title: fragaria vesca var. botryformis
intro: Fragaria vesca var. botryformis, also known as the Alpine Strawberry or Clustered Strawberry, is a rare and unique variety of the wild strawberry plant, characterized by its small, white flowers and edible red fruits that form in clusters. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Fragaria vesca var. botryformis have been linked to several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which can help protect against chronic diseases and promote overall well-being. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is also used in herbal creams, salves, and ointments to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
